description_long: This is a classic urban trolleybus with a streamlined, rectangular body and rounded front and rear ends. The exterior is primarily painted in a combination of red, white, and black, with large windows running along both sides for passenger visibility. On the roof, a prominent yellow pantograph pole is mounted, designed to connect to overhead electric wires for power. The trolleybus features multiple passenger entry doors along its side, as well as detailed elements such as headlights, taillights, and ventilation units on the roof. The undercarriage includes visible wheels and suspension components, while the overall build suggests a robust metal frame with glass windows. The design is typical of mid-to-late 20th-century public transit vehicles found in many European cities.
description: A red, white, and black urban trolleybus with large side windows, roof-mounted pantograph, and multiple passenger doors. Its streamlined body and detailed features evoke classic city public transport.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rounded nose, headlights, and a glimpse of the pantograph on the roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long row of windows, two passenger doors, and the full pantograph structure on the roof.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the rounded back, taillights, and the pantograph base on the roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with windows, doors, and the pantograph visible, mirroring the other side.
